_about: Lexicon for 'A Blind Glass'. Edit freely. Tag a word in a passage with [[word]]; give it a different entry id with [[visible text|id]]. Ids are matched lower-cased. type is 'concept' or 'verb'. Use … for an elision.

system.g: Knowledge ordered more geometrico — as a single deductive whole. Stein gives hers feeling.

resembling.s: Hume, A Treatise of Human Nature
resembling.g: One of the three principles by which the mind associates its ideas.

difference.g: Among the 'greatest kinds' that make it possible to say one thing differs from another.

sight.g: Knowing modelled on seeing; the Good is to the mind what the sun is to the eye.

reason.g: The faculty of principles — and Leibniz's demand that for everything there be a sufficient reason.

negative.g: 'All determination is negation'; for Hegel the negative is what drives the dialectic.

intention.s: Brentano, Psychology from an Empirical Standpoint
intention.g: 'Aboutness' — every mental act is directed at an object.

substance.g: What underlies and persists. Stein lets hers be casually 'replaced'.

wondering.g: 'Philosophy has no other beginning than this.' Iris, the messenger, is daughter of Wonder.

means.g: Sense vs. reference: what an expression says against what it picks out. Stein's 'means' rarely arrives at its Y.

feeling.s: William James, The Principles of Psychology
feeling.g: The continuous stream of consciousness — the grammar Stein learned to write from her teacher.

recognition.g: Self-consciousness won only by being recognised by another.

recurrence.g: To will every moment as returning, identically, forever.

division.g: Cutting a kind at its natural joints to reach its definition.

receptacle.g: The formless 'nurse of all becoming' that receives the Forms.

symbol.g: A thing that stands, for an interpretant, in the place of another.

imitation.g: Art as a copy of a copy, thrice removed from the Form.

act.s: Kant, Groundwork of the Metaphysics of Morals
act.g: 'Act only on a maxim you could will as universal law.' Stein keeps the form and empties the content.

truth.g: Adequation of thought to thing — or, for James, simply what works.

fact.g: The hard particular against which an idea is cashed out and tested.

contradiction.g: The firmest principle of all — or, for Hegel, the very engine of movement. Note Stein's 'both sides and the centre'.

measure.g: 'Of all things the measure is man.'

shows.g: Demonstration: proof that exhibits why a thing must be so. Stein keeps the showing, withholds the proof.

singularity.g: The 'thisness' that makes an individual just this one and no other.

suffering.s: Schopenhauer, The World as Will and Representation
suffering.g: Intrinsic to the striving Will; behind it, Leibniz's problem of evil.

religion.g: Examined first as natural belief, then as varieties of experience.

justice.g: The right ordering of the parts of the soul and of the city.

likeness.g: Becoming like the Forms — and 'becoming like god'.

suppose.g: To posit a ground 'placed under' the argument before it is secured. The master verb of the book.

giving.g: The ethics of giving, receiving, and return — here reduced to a bare oscillation.

passages.title: A Carafe, that is a Blind Glass
passages.text: A kind in glass and a cousin, a spectacle and nothing strange a single hurt color and an arrangement in a [[system]] to pointing. All this and not ordinary, not unordered in not [[resembling]]. The [[difference]] is spreading.

passages.title: A Piece of Coffee
passages.text: The [[sight]] of a [[reason]], the same sight slighter, the sight of a simpler [[negative]] answer, the same sore sounder, the [[intention]] to wishing, the same splendor, the same furniture.

passages.title: A Box
passages.text: So then the order is that a white way of being round is something suggesting a pin and is it disappointing, it is not, it is so rudimentary to be analysed and see a fine [[substance]] strangely.

passages.title: More
passages.text: [[Wondering]] so winningly in several kinds of oceans is the reason that makes red so regular and enthusiastic.

passages.title: A Table
passages.text: A table [[means]] does it not my dear it means a whole steadiness. Is it likely that a change.

passages.title: Roastbeef
passages.text: In [[feeling]] there is resignation, in feeling there is [[recognition]], in feeling there is [[recurrence]] and entirely mistaken there is pinching. … Any time there is a surface there is a surface and every time there is a [[division]] there is a dividing.

passages.title: Mutton
passages.text: Mud and water were not present and not any more of either. … A [[receptacle]] and a [[symbol]] and no monster were present and no more.

passages.title: Breakfast
passages.text: An [[imitation]], more imitation, imitation succeed imitations.

passages.title: Rooms — the opening
passages.text: [[Act so that|act]] there is no use in a centre. A wide action is not a width. … The [[truth]] has come. There is a disturbance. … A [[fact]] is that when the place was replaced all was left that was stored. … There is a [[contradiction]] and naturally returning there comes to be both sides and the centre.

passages.title: Rooms — the singular
passages.text: A [[measure]] is that which put up so that it [[shows]] the length has a steel construction. … is there a [[singularity]], and the singularity, why is there a question. … Why is there so much useless [[suffering]]. Why is there. … A [[religion]], almost a religion, any religion, a quintal in religion.

passages.title: Rooms — the gift
passages.text: The care with which there is incredible [[justice]] and [[likeness]], all this makes a magnificent asparagus, and also a fountain. … [[Suppose]] they are put together, suppose that there is an interruption. … [[Giving it away|giving]], not giving it away, is there any difference.
